revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:飯香岡八幡宮]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Iigaoka Hachimangū | image = [[File:Iigaoka-hachimangu, haiden.jpg|250px|thumb|Haiden of Iigaoka Hachimangū]] | coordinates = {{Coord|35|32|15.1|N|140|7|3.6|E|display=inline,title|region:JP-12_type:landmark}} | location = Yawata 1057-1, Ichihara City, Chiba Prefecture, Japan | deity = * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} (Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) * {{ill|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|en|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|qid=Q3082751|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} * Seven other deities | established = {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11580612}} Era (675 CE, traditional date) | festivals = {{ill|Ghost Festival|en|Ghost Festival|qid=Q696781|lt=Chūgen|1=Ghost Festival}} ({{ill|August 15 (Lunisolar)|ja|8月15日 (旧暦)}} of the lunar calendar) | main hall = {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|qid=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}, copper roof }}
'''Iigaoka Hachimangū''' ({{nihongo|飯香岡八幡宮|いいがおかはちまんぐう}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|en|Ichihara, Chiba|qid=Q207881|lt=Ichihara|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}, Chiba Prefecture, Japan. The shrine is the origin of the local name "Yawata" (八幡), which refers to the area's connection to Hachiman worship. It has been selected as part of the {{ill|500 Sites of Bōsō's Charm|ja|房総の魅力500選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/500 Sites of Bōsō's Charm|1=Best 500 attractions in Chiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11496691}} and {{ill|100 Treasures of Chiba|ja|ちば遺産100選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Treasures of Chiba|qq=}}.
== History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11580612}} Era (675 CE) when the deity {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Emperor Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q317997}} (Emperor Ōjin) was enshrined. Initially known as {{nihongo|Rokusho Mikage Shrine|六所御影神社}}, it was later designated as the primary Hachiman shrine of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|qid=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province|1=Kazusa Province}} under Emperor {{ill|Temmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}}.
From the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the shrine was revered as the provincial Hachiman shrine and gained the honorary title of {{nihongo|"Kōfu Sōsha"|国府総社}} ("Provincial General Shrine"). It played a vital role in rituals such as the {{nihongo|Hōjō-e|放生会}} to pray for peace and prosperity. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, it gained the patronage of prominent samurai clans, including the {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|qid=Q599881|lt=Minamoto clan|1=Minamoto clan}}, {{ill|Chiba clan|en|Chiba clan|qid=Q2975237|lt=Chiba clan|1=Chiba clan}}, and later the {{ill|Tokugawa clan|en|Tokugawa clan|qid=Q1412769|lt=Tokugawa clan|1=Tokugawa clan}}.
The shrine has undergone numerous reconstructions, including contributions by {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|qid=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|1=Ashikaga Takauji}} and {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}. Its current {{ill|Honden|ja|main hall|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was built in the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}} and is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} (Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) * {{ill|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|en|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|qid=Q3082751|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} * Seven auxiliary deities.
== Cultural Properties == [[File:IigaokaHachimangu20110804.jpg|thumb|150px|main hall]] [[File:20240615 Iigaoka Hachimangu no Meoto Icho Ichihara-JPN.jpg|thumb|170px|Meoto Ginkgo trees]] === Nationally Designated Important Cultural Properties === * **main hall (Honden)**: A {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}} structure built in the mid-15th century, designated as an Important Cultural Property in 1954. === Chiba Prefecture Cultural Properties === * **Haiden (Worship Hall)** * **Portable shrines (Mikoshi)**
=== Natural Monuments === * **Meoto Ginkgo Trees**: A pair of ancient ginkgo trees designated as a natural monument by Chiba Prefecture.
== festivals == * **Autumn festival**: Held annually on the {{ill|August 15 (Lunisolar)|ja|8月15日 (旧暦)}} of the lunar calendar. Known for its child-related blessings and traditional rituals. * **Kamidachi festival (神立祭)**: Celebrated on {{ill|September 30 (Lunisolar)|ja|9月30日 (旧暦)}}, praying for good matchmaking and divine intervention from the gods of Izumo.
== Access == * Located at Yawata 1057-1, Ichihara City, Chiba Prefecture. * **Train**: 10-minute walk from {{ill|Yawatajuku Station|en|Yawatajuku Station|qid=Q573497|lt=Yawatajuku Station|1=Yawatajuku Station}} on the {{ill|Uchibō Line|en|Uchibō Line|qid=Q1142964|lt=Uchibō Line|1=Uchibō Line}}.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|qid=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province|1=Kazusa Province}} * {{ill|500 Sites of Bōsō's Charm|ja|房総の魅力500選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/500 Sites of Bōsō's Charm|1=Best 500 attractions in Chiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11496691}} * {{ill|100 Treasures of Chiba|ja|ちば遺産100選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Treasures of Chiba|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.facebook.com/iigaokahatimangu Official Facebook page]
